analysis:
## ⚖️ FACT SUMMARY
Oka addhe-darudu (tenant) tana illu khaali chesin tarvata, illu yajamani (landlord) addhe-darudu icchina security deposit ni tirigi ivvadaniki nirakaristunnaru.

**Key Parties Identified:**
- **Addhe-darudu (Tenant):** Security deposit tirigi pondalani korutunnaru.
- **Illu Yajamani (Landlord):** Security deposit ni tirigi ivvadaniki nirakaristunnaru.

**Key Elements:**
- **Amount/Values involved:** Security deposit amount (specific amount ivvaledu).
- **Location/Jurisdiction indicators:** Jharkhand Tenancy Act mention chesaru, so Jharkhand lo jarigina samasya anukuntunnam.

## 🚨 SEVERITY & URGENCY ASSESSMENT

**Severity Level:** Moderate
**Nature:** Civil
**Urgency:** Time-Sensitive (legal notice pampadaniki, suit veyadaniki time limits undavachu)

⏰ **Time-Sensitive Actions (if any):**
- Illu khaali chesina tarvata, security deposit tirigi ivvadaniki oka reasonable time period untundi. Aa time lo ivvakapothe, ventane legal notice pampadam manchidi.
- State-specific Rent Control Acts lo security deposit recovery ki oka limitation

relevant_sections.title: Return of amount and compensation
relevant_sections.description: If the promoter fails to complete or is unable to give possession of an apartment, plot or building, he shall be liable on demand to the allottees to return the amount received by him in respect of that apartment, plot, building, with interest at such rate as may be prescribed.
relevant_sections.keywords: RERA, real estate, builder, flat, possession, delay, compensation

relevant_sections.title: Grounds for eviction of tenants
relevant_sections.description: Notwithstanding anything contained in any other law, no suit shall be filed for eviction of a tenant from any premises except on one or more of the following grounds: non-payment of rent, subletting without permission, causing damage to premises, requirement for personal use by landlord, premises required for repairs.
relevant_sections.keywords: rent control, eviction, tenant, landlord, non-payment, subletting

relevant_sections.title: Lease defined
relevant_sections.description: A lease of immoveable property is a transfer of a right to enjoy such property, made for a certain time, express or implied, or in perpetuity, in consideration of a price paid or promised, or of money, a share of crops, service or any other thing of value, to be rendered periodically or on specified occasions to the transferor by the transferee, who accepts the transfer on such terms.
relevant_sections.keywords: lease, rent, tenant, landlord, property, rental agreement

relevant_sections.title: Rights of tenant
relevant_sections.description: Tenants have rights to peaceful possession and security of tenure as prescribed.

relevant_sections.act_name: Employees' Provident Fund and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
relevant_sections.title: Penalties
relevant_sections.description: If any employer makes default in the payment of any contribution to the Fund, the Pension Fund or the Insurance Fund, or in the transfer of accumulations required to be transferred by him, or in the payment of any charges payable under any other provision of this Act, he shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years.
relevant_sections.keywords: EPF, PF default, employer contribution, provident fund, pension fund
relevant_sections.punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine up to Rs. 10,000 or both
